articles.title: A former Thiel fellow’s startup just launched a drone it says can replace police helicopters
articles.summary: Brinc launched its newest 911 response drone this week, as the company seeks to expand its prominence in the public safety industry.

articles.title: The BB-777 is the ultimate in boombox nostalgia

articles.summary: The dollar amount isn’t as important as the fact that this is the first jury verdict of its kind against Meta over harm to young people.

articles.title: Lululemon bets Epoch Biodesign can eat its shorts, literally
articles.summary: Startup Epoch Biodesign uses enzymes to break plastic waste down into monomers, the chemical building blocks that can be used to make new plastic.

articles.summary: Though the underlying Sora 2 video- and audio-generation model is scarily impressive, there was not sustained interest in an AI-only social feed.

articles.title: Popular LiteLLM PyPI package backdoored to steal credentials, auth tokens
articles.summary: The TeamPCP hacking group continues its supply-chain rampage, now compromising the massively popular "LiteLLM" Python package on PyPI and claiming to have stolen data from hundreds of thousands of devices during the attack. [...]

articles.title: Meta misled users about its products’ safety, jury decides

articles.title: Anthropic hands Claude Code more control, but keeps it on a leash
articles.summary: Anthropic’s new auto mode for Claude Code lets AI execute tasks with fewer approvals, reflecting a broader shift toward more autonomous tools that balance speed with safety through built-in safeguards.
